name: Vuelta a la Modorra Trail
name_original: Vuelta a la Modorra desde Fuente del Arenazo por cueva del Boquerón y embalse La Toba (PR-CU 109)
description: Hike the challenging 11.0-mile Vuelta a la Modorra Trail through the Serranía de Cuenca, featuring karst geology and the Boquerón Cave.
region_intro: Hiking in Serrania De Cuenca offers diverse experiences across a landscape shaped by rugged mountains, dramatic reliefs, and sandstone gorges. The region is characterized by captivating karstic terrain, formed by water erosion on calcareous rock over millennia. Dense pine forests and an abundance of water contribute to its lush greenery, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails in Serrania De Cuenca The most popular hiking route is El Escalerón to La Raya Trail, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows riverbanks, offering views of the Uña Lagoon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular via Escalerón to La Raya (PR-CU 37), a moderate 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This circular route explores the natural park's varied terrain in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Local hikers also love the Enchanted City Trail, a 1.6 miles (2.5 km) trail leading through surreal limestone formations, often completed in about 40 minutes. Hiking in Serrania De Cuenca is defined by karstic terrain, dense pine forests, and dramatic gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding treks. The routes in Serrania De Cuenca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Serrania De Cuenca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Serranía de Cuenca?
A: Serranía de Cuenca offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls through unique geological formations to more challenging treks across rugged landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on hikes in Serranía de Cuenca?
A: The region is characterized by its captivating karstic terrain, featuring rugged mountains, dramatic reliefs, and sandstone gorges. You'll hike through dense pine forests, along riverbanks, and past unique rock formations sculpted by millennia of water erosion.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Serranía de Cuenca?
A: Yes, Serranía de Cuenca has several easy and family-friendly routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010714930/enchanted-city-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a short, accessible walk through surreal limestone formations. Another gentle option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010700038/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-to-laguna-de-una" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular route to Laguna de Uña&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque views of the lagoon.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: Serranía de Cuenca is rich in natural wonders. You can explore the surreal rock formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City&lt;/a&gt; or the similar 'alleyways' of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Callejones de Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the dramatic panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt;, overlooking the Júcar River canyon. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uña Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; also offers a serene natural setting.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the region?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010706687/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-via-escaleron-to-la-raya-pr-cu-37" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular via Escalerón to La Raya (PR-CU 37)&lt;/a&gt;, which explores varied terrain within the natural park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013912609/tour-of-los-callejones-de-las-majadas-natural-park-of-the-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Los Callejones de Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt; is another circular option, perfect for experiencing the unique karstic formations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Serranía de Cuenca?
A: The most recommended seasons for hiking in Serranía de Cuenca are summer and autumn. Autumn is particularly stunning, as the extensive forests transform into a vibrant display of greens, yellows, and ochres, offering a spectacular backdrop for your walks.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or river features?
A: Absolutely. The region is sustained by an abundance of water, with rivers like the Cuervo, Escabas, and Júcar carving impressive gorges. The Nacimiento del Río Cuervo (Source of the Cuervo River) is a protected natural monument known for its enchanting cascades and crystalline pools, especially beautiful in autumn. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364658118/el-escaleron-to-la-raya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Escalerón to La Raya Trail&lt;/a&gt;, follow these riverbanks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Serranía de Cuenca?
A: The trails in Serranía de Cuenca are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the unique geological formations, and the well-maintained paths that offer a variety of experiences for all levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While many routes are moderate or easy, Serranía de Cuenca does offer more demanding treks that require good physical fitness and sure-footedness. These trails often involve greater elevation changes and cover more rugged terrain, providing a rewarding challenge for experienced hikers.

Q: Is it possible to explore the region using public transport?
A: While some larger towns in the vicinity might be accessible by public transport, many of the trailheads and natural attractions within the Serranía de Cuenca are best reached by car. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and plan accordingly if relying on public options.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
A: Serranía de Cuenca boasts rich biodiversity. Its extensive woodlands are home to deer, wild boars, and mouflons. The skies are patrolled by various raptors, including peregrine falcons, golden eagles, and griffon vultures. El Hosquillo Park also offers opportunities to observe indigenous species like Iberian wolves and brown bears.

Q: Are there any specific items I should bring for hiking in Serranía de Cuenca?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for changing weather, it's recommended to bring sturdy hiking boots, layers of clothing, sufficient water, and sun protection. For longer or more remote trails, a map or GPS device is advisable. During autumn, a camera is a must to capture the vibrant fall colors.
tips: One of the attractions of Monteagudillo is this wonderful cave known as Boquerón, which is located in a fairly remote place on the banks of the Fuencaliente stream. It is accessed by taking a short detour from the marked path PR-CU 108.
The La Toba reservoir dam allows you privileged views of both the reservoir and the Júcar river as it reaches it, crossing a picturesque landscape.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Vuelta a la Modorra Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of medium difficulty, though komoot rates it as 'difficult'. It features a challenging ascent to Modorra hill with narrow and steep sections, and some parts of the path may be less defined. A moderate level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the estimated hiking time for this trail?
faqs.answer: The estimated hiking time for the Vuelta a la Modorra Trail is approximately 5 hours and 11 minutes, covering a distance of about 17.7 kilometers.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead for the Vuelta a la Modorra Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically begins at the Albergue Fuente de las Tablas. The Área de Recreo de la Fuente del Arenazo is also a notable starting point and a good place to pause.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You will encounter a rocky, karst-origin terrain with unique geological formations like lapiaces, ruiniform reliefs, sinkholes, caves, and gorges. The path is largely unpaved, with descents towards the Arroyo del Boquerón, ascents, and some narrow, steep sections.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes by several interesting spots. You can explore the Boquerón Cave and see the impressive La Toba Reservoir Dam.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through a diverse Mediterranean forest, offering impressive natural beauty. You'll have opportunities to observe local wildlife, including various birds and reptiles, thriving in this unique environment.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is suitable for hiking during most of the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, and winter conditions might make some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with other official trails. Approximately 65% of the trail covers sections of the Vuelta a la Modorra. Buenache de la Sierra route, and about 19% aligns with the Monteagudillo y el embalse de la Toba trail.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Vuelta a la Modorra Trail itself.

faqs.question: What are the general regulations for hiking in Serranía de Cuenca?
faqs.answer: When hiking in the Serranía de Cuenca, it's important to stay on marked paths, respect local flora and fauna, and carry out all your waste. Open fires are strictly prohibited, and camping regulations should be observed. Always check for any specific seasonal restrictions or advisories before your visit.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on the Vuelta a la Modorra Trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Serranía de Cuenca, but it is always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.
